note_data.title: Oscillations
keyPoints: **Simple harmonic motion (SHM)** occurs when acceleration is directly proportional to displacement from equilibrium and always directed towards equilibrium position
For SHM: **a = -ω²x** where acceleration is proportional to displacement with negative sign indicating opposite direction
**Displacement-time graph** for SHM is sinusoidal: x = A sin(ωt + φ) or x = A cos(ωt + φ)
**Velocity is maximum** at equilibrium position (x = 0) and **zero at maximum displacement** (x = ±A)
**Kinetic energy is maximum** at equilibrium and **potential energy is maximum** at amplitude, with total energy remaining constant
**Free oscillations** occur at natural frequency with constant amplitude in absence of external forces
**Forced oscillations** occur when external periodic force is applied at driving frequency
**Resonance** occurs when driving frequency equals natural frequency, causing maximum amplitude oscillation
**Light damping** reduces amplitude gradually, **critical damping** returns to equilibrium fastest without overshoot, **heavy damping** returns slowly to equilibrium

definitions.definition: Oscillatory motion where acceleration is directly proportional to displacement from equilibrium position and always directed towards equilibrium
Time taken for one complete oscillation
Phenomenon where amplitude of oscillation becomes maximum when driving frequency equals natural frequency
Process where energy is removed from oscillating system, causing amplitude to decrease with time

howToAnswer.instruction: Give one fact in one sentence. No explanation needed.
Give the point + reason using 'because' or 'this is because'
State what happens in logical sequence without explanation
Draw clearly labelled axes with correct shape and key features marked

modelAnswer.question: A mass attached to a spring oscillates with SHM. Explain why the kinetic energy is maximum when the mass passes through the equilibrium position. [3 marks]
modelAnswer.answer: At equilibrium position, displacement is zero so potential energy is zero. By conservation of energy, when potential energy is minimum, kinetic energy is maximum. Velocity is maximum at equilibrium position because acceleration changes direction at this point.
markBreakdown: Mark 1: At equilibrium, displacement = 0 so PE = 0
Mark 2: Conservation of energy means when PE minimum, KE maximum
Mark 3: Velocity maximum at equilibrium position

commonMistakes: Never say 'force is proportional to displacement' — always say 'acceleration is proportional to displacement'
Students confuse period with frequency — period is time for one oscillation, frequency is oscillations per second
Don't say 'resonance increases frequency' — resonance increases amplitude when frequencies match
Never confuse velocity and acceleration graphs — velocity leads displacement by π/2, acceleration leads by π
Students forget negative sign in a = -ω²x which shows direction towards equilibrium
examinerTips: SHM appears in every Paper 2 — learn the defining equation a = -ω²x
Always sketch displacement, velocity and acceleration on same time axis to show phase relationships
For energy questions, state that total energy = KE + PE = constant
Resonance questions often link to practical examples like Tacoma Bridge collapse
note_data.keyDiagramDescription: Displacement-time graph showing sinusoidal curve with amplitude A and period T marked, plus velocity-time and acceleration-time graphs showing π/2 phase differences
